hypothesis: If a reinforcement learning molecular generative framework incorporates a dual-objective reward function that simultaneously maximizes pharmacophoric similarity (via CATS descriptors) to known estrogen receptor antagonists/degraders while minimizing structural similarity to existing compounds, then it will produce novel drug-like molecules with favorable docking scores, QED values, and synthetic accessibility compared to a baseline docking-only approach, because pharmacophore-guided optimization captures the essential binding feature patterns necessary for biological activity while the structural dissimilarity constraint drives exploration of chemically novel scaffold space.
